mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds a threshold of 3.5%, it signals a restrictive monetary policy regime that increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to lower equity valuations over the medium term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the FEDFUNDS rate is above 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decrease in the unemployment rate signals an improving labor market and robust economic activity. This trend supports higher corporate earnings and positive investor sentiment, leading to positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The information coefficient (IC) of the 3-month change in UNRATE against 20-day forward returns is consistently negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in real retail sales reflects robust consumer demand, which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and positive earnings surprises, boosting equity prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Periods of top-quartile month-over-month RSAFS growth are not followed by statistically significant positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates a contraction in the manufacturing and supply side of the economy, signaling lower future GDP growth and reduced corporate profitability, thus negatively impacting stocks.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in over 50% of the 60-day periods following a trigger event where INDPRO monthly change is less than -0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high inflation (CPI) and low unemployment (UNRATE) forces the central bank into a hawkish stance, leading to aggressive rate hikes that contract valuation multiples and depress equity markets. The signal is only active when both conditions are met.
failure_condition: The dual-trigger condition is not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns on average over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts from a high level, specifically during a monetary tightening cycle, signals a collapse in a key interest-rate sensitive sector, foreshadowing a broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS 6-month change > 1%), a >10% 3-month drop in HOUST is not followed by negative 60-day returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A surprisingly strong nonfarm payrolls report (e.g., >300k job additions) indicates robust, unanticipated economic momentum, leading to upward revisions in earnings expectations and positive short-term market returns.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a >300k PAYEMS print is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.05) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the consumer price index (CPI) signals persistent inflationary pressures, increasing the likelihood of restrictive monetary policy, eroding corporate profit margins, and compressing equity valuation multiples.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in CPIAUCSL and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is positive over any 5-year rolling window.
